DECLARATION OF JOSHUA M DAVIS IN SUPPORT OF DEFT'S REPLY TO PLTFF'S RESPONSE AND OPPOS TO DEFT'S MTN FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION FILED BY DEFENDANT ASHFIELD CAPITAL PARTNERS LLC OLD MUTUAL HOLDINGS INC. OLD MUTUAL ASSET MANAGEMENT

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. AT THE CONCLUSION OF ARGUMENT, THE COURT ADOPTED THE TENTATIVE RULING ON DEFENDANTS ASHFIELD CAPITAL PARTNERS LLC, OLD MUTUAL HOLDINGS INC., AND OLD MUTUAL ASSET MANAGEMENT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. GRANTED AS TO DEFENDANTS OLD MUTUAL HOLDINGS, INC. AND OLD MUTUAL ASSET MANAGEMENT'S (COLLECTIVELY "OLD MUTUAL") MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AND DENIED AS TO ASHFIELD CAPITAL PARTNERS, LLC'S ("ASHFIELD") MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. GRANTED AS TO THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ISSUE NOS. 3 AND 6, AND DENIED AS TO ISSUE NOS. 1, 2, 4, 5. OLD MUTUAL HAS MET ITS INITIAL BURDEN OF PROOF TO SHOW THAT AS THE PARENT COMPANY OF ASHFIELD, OLD MUTUAL DID NOT EMPLOY PLAINTIFF OR WAS RESPONSIBLE FOR PLAINTIFF'S TERMINATION. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT MET HIS SHIFTING BURDEN. ACCORDINGLY, ISSUE NO. 1 IS MOOT IN LIGHT OF THE GRANTING OF OLD MUTUAL'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. FOR ISSUE NO. 2, ASHFIELD HAS NOT MET ITS INITIAL BURDEN OF PROOF TO SHOW THAT IT DID NOT RETALIATE AGAINST PLAINTIFF BY FAILING TO CONSIDER PLAINTIFF FOR OTHER OPEN POSITIONS PRIOR TO OR AFTER TERMINATION. (SEE MORGAN V. REGENTS OF UNIVERSITY OF CAL. (2000) 88 CAL.APP.4TH 52, 67.) FURTHER, BY PROFFERING NEW EVIDENCE IN REPLY TO REBUT PLAINTIFF'S OPPOSITION EVIDENCE, DEFENDANTS HAVE FURTHER DEMONSTRATED THAT THERE ARE TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT ON THE ISSUE OF RETALIATION. FOR ISSUE NOS. 4 AND 5, ASHFIELD'S ARGUMENT IS PREDICATED ON THE GRANTING OF SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AGAINST PLAINTIFF'S RETALIATION AND MISCLASSIFICATION CLAIMS. SINCE THERE ARE TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT REGARDING PLAINTIFF'S RETALIATION CLAIMS, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED. FOR ISSUE NO. 3, DEFENDANT HAS MET ITS INITIAL BURDEN TO DEMONSTRATE THAT PLAINTIFF WAS PROPERLY CLASSIFIED AS AN EXEMPT EMPLOYEE. IN TURN, PLAINTIFF HAS NOT MET HIS SHIFTING BURDEN. FOR ISSUE NO. 6, ASHFIELD HAS MET ITS INITIAL BURDEN TO DEMONSTRATE THAT PLAINTIFF

Off Calendar May-19-2014 Off Calendar

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar May-05-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Joshua M. Davis, Esq. of Goulston & Storrs, PC, Tel. 617.482.1776 and Thomas M. McInerney, of Ogletree Deakins, Tel. 415.442.4810, for defendants and moving party;
Matthew M. Oliveri, Esq. of Oliveri Law Offices, Tel. 925.303.3705, for plaintiff and opposing party, by Court Call.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling on DEFENDANTS ASHFIELD CAPITAL PARTNERS LLC, OLD MUTUAL HOLDINGS INC., and OLD MUTUAL ASSET MANAGEMENT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication. Granted as to Defendants Old Mutual Holdings, Inc. and Old Mutual Asset Management's (collectively "Old Mutual") motion for summary judgment and denied as to Ashfield Capital Partners, LLC's ("Ashfield") motion for summary judgment. Granted as to the motion for summary adjudication Issue nos. 3 and 6, and denied as to Issue nos. 1, 2, 4, 5. Old Mutual has met its initial burden of proof to show that as the parent company of Ashfield, Old Mutual did not employ Plaintiff or was responsible for Plaintiff's termination. Plaintiff has not met his shifting burden. Accordingly, Issue no. 1 is moot in light of the granting of Old Mutual's motion for summary judgment. For Issue no. 2, Ashfield has not met its initial burden of proof to show that it did not retaliate against Plaintiff by failing to consider Plaintiff for other open positions prior to or after termination. (See Morgan v. Regents of University of Cal. (2000) 88 Cal.App.4th 52, 67.) Further, by proffering new evidence in reply to rebut Plaintiff's opposition evidence, Defendants have further demonstrated that there are triable issues of fact on the issue of retaliation. For Issue nos. 4 and 5, Ashfield's argument is predicated on the granting of summary adjudication against Plaintiff's retaliation and misclassification claims. Since there are triable issues of fact regarding Plaintiff's retaliation claims, summary adjudication is denied. For Issue no. 3, Defendant has met its initial burden to demonstrate that Plaintiff was properly classified as an exempt employee. In turn, Plaintiff has not met his shifting burden. For Issue no. 6, Ashfield has met its initial burden to demonstrate that Plaintiff cannot establish his defamation claim. In turn, Plaintiff has not met his shifting burden. (Counsel for plaintiff to submit a proposed order that complies with CCP 437c(g) and CRC 3.1312.) Judge: A. James Robertson, II; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR# 5976.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
